key_insight: PragReST boosts LLM pragmatic understanding by self-generating counterfactual reasoning traces and training models to prefer implied meanings over literal ones.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, label-free way to improve real-world language agents on inference tasks while preserving general reasoning performance.
method: Constructs pragmatic QA data with self-supervised counterfactual examples
Trains via supervised fine-tuning plus reinforcement learning
Avoids human labels and teacher-model distillation
evidence_signal: Evaluated on four pragmatic benchmarks (PragMega, Ludwig, MetoQA, AltPrag), PragReST outperformed backbones and baselines, improving accuracy by 5.37% and 5.50% for Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-14B on accuracy-based tasks.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality counterfactual reasoning traces, and removing that component substantially reduces gains.
paper_title: PragReST: Self-Reinforcing Counterfactual Reasoning for Pragmatic Language Understanding
